summary: Tested the new Readiness Plan lead-capture flow (backend POST/GET /api/leads + 7-step frontend questionnaire) and hero/CTA copy updates. All 26 test cases passed across backend pytest (7), readiness-page UI (11), and home/hero/CTA/mobile (8).
critical_code_review_comments: ReadinessPlanPage.jsx is ~520 lines — still manageable but approaching the 700-line guideline. Consider extracting INDUSTRIES/SIZES/PAINS/INTERESTS/TOOLS/SPEND constants to a separate config file and the per-step JSX into small components if more steps/fields are added.

GET /api/leads silently returns [] for an invalid/missing admin_token. Functionally what was requested, but from an API hygiene perspective returning HTTP 401 would be cleaner and less confusing for future integrators. Document the current behaviour in the API docs at minimum.

ADMIN_TOKEN default is the placeholder 'change-me-to-a-random-string' committed in backend/.env — make sure this is rotated to a secure value before production deploy. Consider failing fast if ADMIN_TOKEN is empty or equals this placeholder.

Frontend email validation uses regex /\S+@\S+\.\S+/ — backend has no validation on email format. Consider using pydantic EmailStr (requires email-validator package) so server rejects malformed emails before they hit Mongo.

Lead model echoes back PII (name/email/phone) directly to the client which is fine for the submitter, but ensure GET /api/leads is hardened later — currently rate-limit-free and only protected by a query-param token (vulnerable to URL/log leakage). Move admin_token to an Authorization header or proper admin auth for production.
test_credentials: ADMIN_TOKEN='change-me-to-a-random-string' (read from backend/.env). No user credentials needed.
seed_data_creation: Pytest creates 'TEST_'-prefixed leads in MongoDB on each run (not auto-cleaned). The GET-with-admin-token test verifies they appear in the admin list.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All readiness-plan flows verified end-to-end: POST /api/leads, GET /api/leads (auth gated), 7-step form with progress bar, all chip test-ids (industry-*, size-*, pain-0..7, interest-0..7, tool-0..11, spend-0..5), single-select on pain step, multi-select on interest step, back/next/submit nav, done screen with done-home + done-examples, secondary CTAs (readiness-calendly, readiness-whatsapp), readiness-exit -> '/'. Hero copy verified to contain 'A standalone website? That's so 2018', 'lower costs', 'put AI to work'. CTAs hero-readiness-btn/hero-calendly-btn/hero-services-btn, ai-ready-readiness/ai-ready-calendly, ai-frustration-readiness/ai-frustration-calendly all linked correctly. Existing routes /work /services /process /contact /examples still load. Mobile 375px has no horizontal overflow.
